# Volt Nederland Platform — Four Priority Areas

## Summary

Volt Nederland's 2025 election programme ("Toekomst, nu") sets out positions across all four of the user's priority areas. On climate, the party holds one of the most ambitious targets in the Dutch field: legally binding climate neutrality by 2040 (ten years ahead of the Paris-default 2050, five years ahead of D66's trajectory). On EU integration and defense, Volt is the most structurally federalist party in the Dutch parliament, advocating for a genuine European army under parliamentary oversight and a path toward a federal European constitution. On housing and taxation, the programme takes progressive-redistributive positions — abolishing mortgage interest deduction as quickly as possible, a wealth tax from €57,000, and European-level housing regulation. On democracy and digital rights, the programme contains specific institutional proposals (constitutional court, citizen assembly, lobby register, algorithm register) that are both more detailed and more radical than comparable parties.

The 2025 programme updates the 2021 and 2023 versions with heightened urgency on defense (driven by US disengagement signals) and adjustments to housing affordability framing. Volt also ran a 2024 EU election campaign programme that broadly mirrors but deepens the national positions on EU federalism.

## Evidence

### Climate and Energy
- [Volt verkiezingsprogramma klimaat samenvatting — NVDE](https://www.nvde.nl/samenvatting-volt-verkiezingsprogramma-klimaat-energie/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Most detailed public summary of Volt's energy platform. Key points: 2040 climate neutrality legally binding; ban on combustion engine sales by 2030; small modular reactors (SMRs) alongside renewables; Contract-for-Differences for wind; eliminate all fossil fuel subsidies and tax exceptions; CO2 pricing revenue channelled into decarbonisation; opposition to new oil/gas drilling; usage-based vehicle taxation replacing fixed levies; kerosene taxation and aviation levies.
- [Ikformeer.nl — Volt standpunten 2025](https://ikformeer.nl/tweede-kamerverkiezingen-2025/partij/volt) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Party comparison portal. Confirms Volt supports new offshore wind, more nuclear plants, opposes loosening EU climate rules, wants to stop all gas and oil extraction, and targets halving livestock numbers by 2030 with nature connectivity requirements.
- [Volt verkiezingsprogramma 2025 overview — Volt Nederland](https://voltnederland.org/verkiezingsprogramma-2025) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Landing page listing six thematic areas: green environment, good foundations, equality, innovative economy, Europe in a safe world, democracy. The PDF itself was too large to retrieve via automated fetch; detail drawn from NVDE summary and ikformeer.nl.

### EU Integration and Defense
- [Een Europese krijgsmacht — Volt Nederland](https://voltnederland.org/standpunten/ga-voor-europa-in-een-veilige-wereld/een-europese-krijgsmacht) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Primary source for defense policy. Specific proposals: unified European command structure with Brussels HQ; Rapid Deployment Capacity expanding to 60,000+ personnel; centralized military academy; shared intelligence service; defense procurement consolidation; spending above 2% GDP directed toward joint EU projects via Eurobonds; French nuclear modernisation toward independent European deterrence under democratic control; no-first-use policy. Overtly frames US disengagement as justification.
- [Volt: Minder afhankelijk van VS, 2% vastleggen — Defensieplatform](https://www.defensieplatform.nl/volt-minder-afhankelijk-van-vs-2-vastleggen-en-naar-europees-leger/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Secondary source confirming legal binding of 2% NATO norm, Rapid Response Force, EU military Erasmus exchange, joint equipment procurement.
- [Volt Europa Policies](https://volteuropa.org/policies) (accessed 2026-04-19) — European-level programme: elect EU Prime Minister; grant European Parliament legislative initiative; EU Federal Declaration path; abolish Council veto; transnational electoral lists.
- [Volt EU campaign programme 2024 Dutch (PDF metadata only)](https://volteuropa.org/storage/pdf/eu-elections-2024/volt_campaignprogramme_dutch.pdf) (accessed 2026-04-19) — PDF was not machine-readable in this fetch; content drawn from secondary sources.
- [Brusselse Nieuwe — European defense policy party comparison](https://brusselsenieuwe.nl/wat-zeggen-politieke-partijen-in-hun-verkiezingsprogramma-over-het-europese-defensiebeleid/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Cross-party comparison: Volt supports EU military force cooperating with NATO; spending above 2% directed to EU projects. Confirms Volt is the only party explicitly linking surplus defense spending to European integration mechanisms.
- [Ga voor Europa in een veilige wereld — Volt Nederland](https://voltnederland.org/standpunten/ga-voor-europa-in-een-veilige-wereld) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Landing page for Volt's security chapter. Ukraine: supports Coalition of the Willing, wants to fill gaps left by US withdrawal.

### Housing, Economy, and Taxation
- [Viisi — verkiezingsprogramma's 2025 woningmarkt](https://www.viisi.nl/nieuws/verkiezingsprogrammas-2025-woningmarkt-en-hypotheekrenteaftrek-per-partij/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Cross-party housing comparison. Volt: abolish mortgage interest deduction as quickly as possible, shift own-home to wealth box; cost-sharing norm abolished; minimum two-thirds affordable units in new construction (≥30% social); government assumes sustainability costs for retrofits; argues housing crisis is a European-level problem.
- [Ikformeer.nl — Volt standpunten 2025](https://ikformeer.nl/tweede-kamerverkiezingen-2025/partij/volt) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Tax proposals: wealth tax on assets above €57,000; income-dependent fines; inheritance tax converted to receipt-based tax treating inheritances as regular income; eliminate fossil fuel subsidies; increased flight taxes.

### Democracy and Digital Rights
- [Een sterk democratisch Nederland — Volt Nederland](https://voltnederland.org/standpunten/ga-voor-democratie/een-sterk-democratisch-nederland) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Primary source. Key proposals: remove Article 120's prohibition on judicial review (enabling a constitutional court function); expand Tweede Kamer from 150 to 250 seats; create world's first permanent national citizen deliberation council; mandatory lobby register (Irish model); mandatory public disclosure of government officials' financial interests; automatic ministerial communications archiving; Rule of Law Planning Bureau; minimum GDP allocation for independent courts; judge appointment insulated from ministerial discretion; lower voting age to 16; digital voting pilot for Dutch nationals abroad; Party Act requiring internal democratic standards.
- [Digitale democratie — Volt Nederland](https://voltnederland.org/standpunten/ga-voor-democratie/digitale-democratie) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Digital democracy standpoints page.
- [Digitaliseringstoets — Volt Nederland](https://voltnederland.org/standpunten/digitale-zaken/digitale-toets-en-algoritmeregister) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Algorithm register: mandatory public record of which algorithms are deployed by government, how they operate, what data they process; rights-based testing for all AI in public decision-making; broader "digitaliseringstoets" for new legislation.
- [Waarborgen van privacy — Volt Nederland](https://voltnederland.org/standpunten/digitale-zaken/waarborgen-van-privacy) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Privacy standpoints: personal data belongs to the individual; invest in enforcement of European frameworks; oppose large-scale real-time facial recognition; mandatory human-rights test for security legislation; ban on ethnic profiling in automated risk systems.
- [Ikformeer.nl — Volt standpunten 2025](https://ikformeer.nl/tweede-kamerverkiezingen-2025/partij/volt) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Confirms: oppose facial recognition cameras for mass surveillance; oppose tracking protest attendance; reject ethnic/nationality-based algorithmic crime-risk profiling; support constitutional review by judges; oppose royal appointment powers in coalition formation; easier citizenship acquisition.

## Uncertainty / conflicting sources

- **2025 programme PDF inaccessible**: The full 2025 election programme PDF at `voltnederland.org/storage/doc/volt_verkiezingsprogramma_2025.pdf` exceeded the automated fetch size limit and could not be retrieved in full. Detail on housing and taxation is pieced together from NVDE summaries, ikformeer.nl, and party standpoints pages. There may be additional nuance in the full text.
- **Nuclear energy ambiguity**: Volt's 2025 programme supports SMRs (small modular reactors) and does not oppose nuclear power, which is unusual for a progressive party. The NVDE summary and ikformeer.nl both confirm this. Whether this is in tension with Volt's broader environmental commitments is not addressed in available sources.
- **Wealth tax threshold**: The €57,000 figure is from ikformeer.nl's summary of the 2025 programme. This is a relatively low threshold (encompassing many middle-class savings); whether this is a net-asset or gross-asset definition is unclear from available sources.
- **2021 vs 2025 programme continuity**: The 2021 programme (PDF archived on Volt's site) is available; the 2025 programme is the operative one. Where the two programmes differ materially (e.g., on defense urgency), the 2025 version is used.
